channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - New Orleans Pelicans
channel.description: New Orleans Pelicans trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: Bucks leaving door open to acquire superstar in trade to convince Giannis Antetokounmpo to stay
item.description: The Bucks are also leaving the door open to pursue a major trade to convince Antetokounmpo to stay once again, allowing Horst to work parallel paths. They will have up to three first-round picks and tradeable contracts such as Bobby Portis and Kyle Kuzma to offer in deals this offseason. One of those first-rounders is the less favorable of their own or the New Orleans Pelicans' this year.

item.title: 2019: Pelicans trade Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: 2019: Pelicans trade Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft. 2025: Mavericks trade for Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft. 2026: Wizards trade for Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft

item.title: Bucks leaving door open to acquire superstar in trade to convince Giannis Antetokounmpo to stay
item.description: The Bucks are also leaving the door open to pursue a major trade to convince Antetokounmpo to stay once again, allowing Horst to work parallel paths. They will have up to three first-round picks and tradeable contracts such as Bobby Portis and Kyle Kuzma to offer in deals this offseason. One of those first-rounders is the less favorable of their own or the New Orleans Pelicans' this year.

item.title: 2019: Pelicans trade Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: 2019: Pelicans trade Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft. 2025: Mavericks trade for Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft. 2026: Wizards trade for Anthony Davis, win the No. 1 pick in the draft

item.title: I've been saying for over a week that I believe Steve …
item.description: Jake Madison: While I don't have a specific date or timeline my understanding is we are close to the end of the Pelicans head coaching search. I've been saying for over a week that I believe Steve Hetzel is the choice and still think that. Jamahl Mosley also remains a possibility

item.title: Collins was able to complete the initial parts of his …
item.description: Collins chose a treatment plan that he felt would give him the best quality of life, while also giving him a chance to extend his life beyond the initial prognosis. Collins was able to complete the initial parts of his treatment plan but the cancer returned too quickly for him to complete all of it. He is survived by his husband, Brunson Green, parents Portia and Paul Collins, and brother, Jarron Collins, who was most recently an assistant coach with the New Orleans Pelicans.

item.title: The Warriors are in the early stages of searching for …
item.description: The Warriors are in the early stages of searching for replacements for Stotts and Stackhouse. Former New Orleans Pelicans head coach Willie Green is among the candidates, league sources said. Green started his NBA coaching career with the Warriors in 2016 and spent three seasons as an assistant on Kerr's staff. The two remain close.

item.title: Pelicans will hire a head coach within the next couple days?
item.description: Will Guillory: There's been some chatter about how much time the Pelicans have taken to finalize their decision on a new head coach. I'd expect them to wrap up the process early this week.

item.title: New Orleans has held a longstanding affinity for …
item.description: The widely presumed explanation circulating at the Chicago draft combine this week: New Orleans has held a longstanding affinity for former Magic coach Jamahl Mosley — as The People's Insider Jake Fischer first reported here in November — and is believed to be taking as much time as possible to allow for Mosley to become a full-fledged prime candidate for the post after his recent dismissal in Orlando.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama, Spurs to headline NBA’s Europe regular-season games in France and England
item.description: The NBA's two regular season games in Europe next January will be in Paris and Manchester and The Stein Line has learned that Victor Wembanyama's San Antonio Spurs — for obvious reasons — have been selected to headline the trip. Spurs vs. Pelicans, I'm told, is the expected pairing for the two-game series.

item.title: New Orleans Pelicans to hire Jamahl Mosley as new head coach on five-year deal
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: The New Orleans Pelicans are hiring Jamahl Mosley as the franchise's new head coach on a five-year contract, sources tell ESPN. Mosley accepts the Pelicans job after five seasons in Orlando where he guided the Magic to three consecutive playoff berths.

item.title: Pelicans to hire Jamahl Mosley as new head coach, both sides agree to five-year deal
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: The New Orleans Pelicans are hiring Jamahl Mosley as the franchise's new head coach on a five-year contract, sources tell ESPN. Mosley accepts the Pelicans job after five seasons in Orlando where he guided the Magic to three consecutive playoff berths.

item.title: The Pelicans believe the franchise is at a point …
item.description: Shams Charania: Sources said top Pelicans officials maintained consistent communication with Mosley since he was dismissed by the Magic on May 4 and the sides formally met in person last week at the NBA draft combine in Chicago. The Pelicans believe the franchise is at a point similar to Mosley's leadership and development plan when he took over the rebuilding Magic in 2021 after a 21-win season. It led to season-by-season improvement, then playoff appearances. In Orlando, Mosley built teams anc

item.title: From the beginning since November, Mosley was the …
item.description: Shamit Dua: From the beginning since November, Mosley was the presumed favorite for the job. Internal and external people both felt it was his job to lose. Pelicans get the coach they have long held in high regard

item.title: The Pelicans just formally announced Jamahl Mosley as …
item.description: Marc Stein: The Pelicans just formally announced Jamahl Mosley as their new head coach.

item.title: New Orleans Pelicans Governor Gayle Benson on hiring …
item.description: Mark Medina: New Orleans Pelicans Governor Gayle Benson on hiring Jamahl Mosley as head coach.

item.title: “Jamahl has earned tremendous respect across the NBA …
item.description: Will Guillory: Joe Dumars' statement on the Jamahl Mosley hire: “Jamahl has earned tremendous respect across the NBA for his leadership, professionalism, and the strong relationships he develops with players and staff. He has consistently demonstrated an ability to develop young talent while establishing teams that compete with toughness, discipline, and togetherness. His teams reflect his coaching style through their defensive intensity, effort, preparation, and commitment to playing the right

item.title: I'm told that Mosley has touched down in New Orleans …
item.description: Shamit Dua: I'm told that Mosley has touched down in New Orleans and the introduction presser will be soon

item.title: Pelicans will introduce Jamahl Mosley on Tuesday May …
item.description: Shamit Dua: Pelicans will introduce Jamahl Mosley on Tuesday May 26th

item.title: Jamahl Mosley was among those the Warriors were …
item.description: Anthony Slater: Jamahl Mosley was among those the Warriors were looking at for their vacant lead assistant job, but he lands another HC opportunity with the Pelicans

item.title: Largest fourth quarter comeback playoffs wins in the …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Largest fourth quarter comeback playoffs wins in the play-by-play era: Clippers, down 24 vs MEM in 2012 Knicks, down 22 vs CLE tonight Celtics, down 21 vs NJN in 2002 Warriors, down 20 vs NOP in 2015

item.title: The NBA announced details for the Spurs trip to Europe …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: The NBA announced details for the Spurs trip to Europe next season for 2 games vs. New Orleans. . The Paris game will be Jan. 14 at Accor Arena. The game in Manchester, England, will be Jan. 17 at Co-op Live. Additional info, including ticket sales, will be announced later.

item.title: Pelicans keeping Zion Williamson?
item.description: Chris Mannix: "The big question moving forward in New Orleans now is will Zion Williamson be part of this next iteration of Pelicans teams? From what I was told during this interview process with coaches, the plan is to hold on to Zion, to keep him in New Orleans. He's got two full years left on his contract. And maybe one of the silver linings in a disappointing 25-26 season was Zion was relatively healthy. He played 62 games, averaged right around 21 points per game, shot a good percentage fro

item.title: "When Jamahl Mosley became available, I was told that …
item.description: Chris Mannix: "When Jamahl Mosley became available, I was told that the Pelicans shifted their interest quickly and immediately to Jamahl Mosley. Jamahl has a history of taking over young developing teams and turning them into winners. His first year in Orlando, the Magic won 22 games, they won 47 two years later. Now, they were never really able to build on that to become the kind of sustainable contender that Orlando fans, the Orlando organization had hoped for."

item.title: The Spurs and Pelicans will play in Europe next …
item.description: ‪Mike Vorkunov‬: The Spurs and Pelicans will play in Europe next season. A game in Paris on Thursday, Jan. 14, 2027, and at Co-op Live in Manchester, England, on Sunday, Jan. 17, 2027

item.title: What about New Orleans for Gobert? Toronto. They were …
item.description: Darren Wolfson: "I just I've always come to the conclusion, if Rudy Gobert is playing 30 to 35 minutes a night, you're not winning a championship. I've thought that for years, I continue to think that, but he's an incredible floor raiser. Like if you're Charlotte, he would be great. If you're New Orleans, think about hiring defensive-minded head coach Jamal Mosley. What about New Orleans for Gobert? Toronto. They were good, but Jakob Poeltl, like no. Toronto's had interest in Gobert for a while.

item.title: 2025-26 Kia NBA All-Rookie Second Team:
item.description: NBA Communications: The 2025-26 Kia NBA All-Rookie Second Team: Ace Bailey, @utahjazz Jeremiah Fears, @PelicansNBA Collin Murray-Boyles, @Raptors Derik Queen, @PelicansNBA Maxime Raynaud, @SacramentoKings

item.title: New Pelicans head coach Jamahl Mosley’s first meal in …
item.description: Fletcher Mackel: New Pelicans head coach Jamahl Mosley’s first meal in New Orleans. Desi Vega’s Steakhouse

item.title: Rival executives who’ve spoken with HoopsHype project …
item.description: Following the NBA Draft Lottery, the Atlanta Hawks landed the eighth pick in the draft via the New Orleans Pelicans from the Derik Queen trade. Since then, rival executives who’ve spoken with HoopsHype project the Hawks to select either a point guard, with one of Keaton Wagler, Mikel Brown Jr., Darius Acuff, and Kingston Flemings expected to be available, or Michigan 7-foot-3 center Aday Mara.

item.title: Jamahl Mosley’s coaching staff is expected to include …
item.description: Jamahl Mosley’s coaching staff is expected to include Pelicans holdovers Greg Monroe, Jodie Meeks, Mike Hopkins, and Will Bynum, league sources told HoopsHype. Additionally, Dale Osbourne and Bret Brielmaier, who were both a part of Mosley’s assistant coaching staff with the Orlando Magic, are strong candidates to join Mosley in New Orleans, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: God Shammgod joining Pelicans as an assistant coach
item.description: Michael Scotto: God Shammgod has agreed to join the New Orleans Pelicans as an assistant coach on the front of the bench for head coach Jamahl Mosley, league sources told @hoopshype. Shammgod was an assistant coach with Mosley for the Magic and with Carlisle and Kidd on the Mavericks.

item.title: All of that said, it also would not be the first time …
item.description: So, effectively, Heat management is putting all their eggs in the Giannis Antetokounmpo basket up until draft night, when Bucks management has thankfully set a deadline for Giannis deal or no deal? Then if it’s no Giannis for whatever reason, the Heat have lots of time to go after others by trade or free agency. – Morgan, New Orleans. Ira Winderman: Exactly. Or to go with a Plan C, and wait for the 2027 offseason, when ample cap space can be created. And that makes sense, since the last thing th

item.title: Lakers hiring Rohan Ramadas as assistant general manager
item.description: Shams Charania: The Los Angeles Lakers are hiring former New Orleans Pelicans vice president of strategy and operations Rohan Ramadas as an assistant general manager under president Rob Pelinka, sources tell me and @mcten. The Lakers have interviewed candidates to bolster their front office.

item.title: When you go into your next job after having the …
item.description: What was your message to him when he was fired by the Magic? JB Bickerstaff: We talk about everything all the time. I told him that his next job was going to be his best job. When you get a job as a first-time coach, you’re kind of at the organization’s whim because they have to choose you. When you go into your next job after having the success that he had, you get an opportunity to choose. You get an opportunity to ask questions that first-time head coaches don’t get to ask. It’s important tha

item.title: His willingness to sacrifice of himself for the …
item.description: What makes Mosley a good coach? JB Bickerstaff: The way that he cares about the people he works with. His willingness to sacrifice of himself for the greater good and for the players he’s coaching and the organization as a whole. I think that’s where it starts. But then you look at his teams and his teams are always well coached. Defensively, they are always really good, really sound. Offensively, they had a plan. You had to game-plan to beat his teams. His teams didn’t beat themselves.

item.title: “We're warming up, layup lines, and I shoot my layup …
item.description: On “Young Man & the Three,” Murphy recalled a “really crazy” story about playing against Ricky Rubio in a tiny gym in Spain during his freshman year in college in 2018. “We're warming up, layup lines, and I shoot my layup and go back to the other line where you're about to rebound. I look on the other end, and I see this dude in a full-arm (tattoo) sleeve. I'm like, ‘He kind of looks like Ricky Rubio. But why would Ricky Rubio be playing against Rice University right now?'” said Murphy.

item.title: “So I'm like, ‘If he's hitting seven threes against …
item.description: After their coach confirmed that Rubio, who was then with the Utah Jazz, was suiting up, Murphy admitted that he had a reality check. “I haven't played one college game yet. I'm about to play against Ricky Rubio. He hit seven threes. The thing was, he wasn't that great of a shooter in the league,” added Murphy. “So I'm like, ‘If he's hitting seven threes against us, what does that mean for me?' I was amazed by that. I can't lie to you. It was crazy. He made us look like we were kids.”

item.title: Jamahl Mosley says Pelicans will be 'unapologetically physical'
item.description: Rod Walker: Jamahl Mosley: “We are going to be unapologetically physical.” #Pelicans

item.title: "Accountability" and "physicality" are two words that …
item.description: Will Guillory: "Accountability" and "physicality" are two words that Mosley has mentioned multiple times during his opening presser. Feels like that's what he wants to really emphasize coming into this situation.

item.title: Former #Magic coach Jamahl Mosley shouted out God …
item.description: Jason Beede: Former #Magic coach Jamahl Mosley shouted out God Shammgod, Bret Brielmaier, Dale Osbourne, and Randy Gregory as members of his coaching staff in attendance for his introductory press conference with the Pelicans in New Orleans.

item.title: New Pelicans coach Jamahl Mosley: Zion Williamson hasn't even scratched the surface of what he can do
item.description: Ohm Youngmisuk: Jamahl Mosley says Zion Williamson hasn't even "scratched the surface of what he can do." He wants to open the floor for Zion and says health is also a major key to unlocking Zion further.

item.title: Joe Dumars on Jamahl Mosley: "Jamahl was the person …
item.description: Will Guillory: Joe Dumars on Jamahl Mosley: "Jamahl was the person who aligned with us and our vision more than anyone else."

item.title: Derik Queen, Yves Missi and Micah Peavy are among the …
item.description: Will Guillory: Derik Queen, Yves Missi and Micah Peavy are among the players here for Jamahl Mosley's intro press conference

item.title: "Yeah, give me a day or two though. I am just trying …
item.description: Pelicans Film Room: "When they lost only then did I reach out to him, the next day... I texted him "Should we have a conversation?" and he said "Yeah, give me a day or two though. I am just trying to breath right now" That was the first time I reached out to him" -- Joe Dumars on Jamahl Mosley

item.title: "We haven't changed our opinions on Zion.
item.description: Pelicans Film Room: "We haven't changed our opinions on Zion. I'd love to see Jamahl coach Zion. I'd love to see him get Zion to defend and do all the things he said. Lets see what we have here" -- Joe Dumars on Zion Williamson

item.title: "I think just opening the floor up a lot more for him …
item.description: "I think just opening the floor up a lot more for him to attack the basket, giving space, being able to live at the free throw line consistently," Mosley said of how he wants to capitalize on Williamson's skill set. "Some of the things he's done here have been obviously spectacular -- and so just making sure that we continue that along with us being healthy."

item.title: [Queen] was talking about the intensity like "I …
item.description: Pelicans Film Room: "About half the team have been to games. They wanted to see it and feel it... [Queen] was talking about the intensity like "I haven't seen that in the regular season" I said "You're not going to see that in the regular season" -- Joe Dumars on players attending playoff games

item.title: It's when you find a way to do the small things better …
item.description: Pelicans Film Room: "It's in the small things you do every day, that's greatness, that's a winning culture. It's when you find a way to do the small things better than anyone else... When you dominate the simple, that changes how you do everything" -- Jamahl Mosley's goals for this season

item.title: On average, top-five picks from 2000 through 2019 …
item.description: The dream of a decade-plus of production for a franchise cornerstone also doesn't materialize very often. On average, top-five picks from 2000 through 2019 lasted 5.1 seasons with their original team, with 56% lasting until their fifth year (and their second contract) and 44% leaving before then. Only 6% played at least 10 seasons with their initial team. (Tatum will bump that figure to 7% next season.) And for now, only five of the 100 top-five picks in that period are still with the team that

item.title: Simmons on the Pelicans trading Trey Murphy: “We …
item.description: Garland Gillen: Bill Simmons on the Pelicans trading Trey Murphy: “We assume he’s probably going to get traded over the next three weeks. I think Boston is a possibility. We thought whoever lost this matchup (Spurs and Thunder) is a possibility. You have two to three weeks to trade for someone like that.”

item.title: Rick Adelman (1946-2026)
item.description: NBA Coaches Assoc.: The membership of the National Basketball Coaches Association joins the NBA family in mourning the passing of legendary Head Coach and Hall of Famer, Rick Adelman. Rick Adelman coached in the NBA for 29 years, serving as a Head Coach for 23 seasons with the Portland Trail Blazers, Golden State Warriors, Sacramento Kings, Houston Rockets and Minnesota Timberwolves. Over his coaching career, Rick won 1,042 games (10th all-time), was inducted into the Naismith Basketball Hall of

item.title: Just want to make sure I'm on record saying any talk …
item.description: Will Guillory: Just want to make sure I'm on record saying any talk of trading Trey Murphy is absolute insanity

item.title: To my understanding there has been no discussion …
item.description: Shamit Dua: To my understanding there has been no discussion between Boston and New Orleans on Jaylen Brown as of this moment. I do believe the Pelicans will be aggressive in reshaping their roster this summer and it's possible that Brown is name they check in on. But I don't believe the Pelicans have plans to mortgage their future in terms of significant draft capital in any moves.

item.title: Marshall is close friends with Knicks guard Jose …
item.description: Mike Curtis: #Mavs forward Naji Marshall is in San Antonio for tonight’s Game 1 between the #Knicks and #Spurs. Marshall is close friends with Knicks guard Jose Alvarado, his former teammate of three seasons in New Orleans.

item.title: Chicago Bulls are adding Alex Kaufman as Windy City …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Chicago Bulls are adding Alex Kaufman as Windy City Bulls general manager and Jarrett Sutton as a scout, league sources told @hoopshype. Kaufman and Sutton worked with Bulls executive Bryson Graham for six years with the New Orleans Pelicans.

item.title: The Pelicans have named David Cosgrave as Vice …
item.description: New Orleans Pelicans: The Pelicans have named David Cosgrave as Vice President of Health & Performance and Joe Sharpe as Head Athletic Trainer Cosgrave spent over 12 years in the Premier League, contributing across performance environments at Tottenham Hotspur, Liverpool FC, Stoke City, and Fulham FC. He later played a key role at FC Copenhagen during a highly successful period, contributing to six Danish titles and multiple deep UEFA competition runs between 2014 and 2021. Sharpe brings over

item.title: “It felt like no matter how good of a job they did, or …
item.description: Furthering this initial tension, when Cosgrave was first hired as a consultant, his specific role and responsibilities were not broadly communicated within the organization. This created some uncertainty among staff about his purpose and scope. It is a dynamic the organization has since acknowledged. By the time the season ended, many on the staff believed their fate had been sealed regardless of their performance. “It felt like no matter how good of a job they did, or how much the players liked

item.title: The organization has made clear Dumars has no …
item.description: The organization has made clear Dumars has no intention of being constrained by budget in rebuilding the medical and performance department, and is prepared to spend what is required to bring in the caliber of people the organization is targeting. The hires of Cosgrave and Sharpe reflect that commitment.

item.title: “The goal is to elevate the overall standard of …
item.description: The Pelicans began sweeping changes at the end of the season, parting ways with the majority of their performance staff, with a few staffers opting to leave independently for other opportunities. The restructuring spanned athletic training, strength and conditioning, performance science, mental health, nutrition and rehabilitation. In the interim, the team’s G-League affiliate training staff has been brought in to assist with player care while the full hiring process continues — a practice sourc

item.title: "Trey Murphy’s still going to be a name that we’re …
item.description: Jake Fischer revealed the latest revelation around the highly sought Pelicans wing on his Tuesday Bleacher Report livestream. "Trey Murphy’s still going to be a name that we’re going to talk about and write about on the Stein Line, because other teams absolutely want him. The New Orleans Pelicans, though, of course, shut down pretty much all interest in him in February, and they didn’t really want a picks-laden package," Fischer bared. "I think if the Pelicans were to move Trey Murphy III, it wo

item.title: Trae Young: "I can play multiple ways.
item.description: Trae Young: "I can play multiple ways. A lot of people just see what I've done and me being ball-dominant. I can't wait to show other things. My boy Dejounte Murray, who's with me 24/7, he was my point guard for a lot of years and I was just a shooter. I didn't do nothing but just shoot the ball. So I can't wait to show that. But also, yes, you can win with a smaller guard, of course. It's been done before."

item.title: Pelicans trying to trade into 2026 draft lottery
item.description: After trading into the lottery last year to select Derik Queen, the New Orleans are trying to trade into the lottery again in the upcoming 2026 NBA Draft, league sources told HoopsHype. The Pelicans are believed to be targeting a specific player in the top-10 range.

item.title: Pistons, Pacers pursuing Trey Murphy
item.description: External trade interest in New Orleans' Trey Murphy III remains intense, league sources say. Yet I was told this week that the inquiries have all been of an inbound nature. League sources say that the Pelicans have not offered Murphy anywhere and that this is the first time their new front office regime headed by Joe Dumars has been truly willing to field offers for Murphy. As one team interested in Murphy put it to me this week: "They want a lot." Two teams known to have expressed interest in t

item.title: “Very punctual. Very consistent on a day-to-day basis. …
item.description: It'll take plenty of work, but Thomas Scott is willing to put that work in. He has seen hard work all of his life watching his dad. It’s one of the things his father passed down to him. “I’ve always observed my dad being very disciplined and always moving as a professional,” he said. “Very punctual. Very consistent on a day-to-day basis. Somebody that knew how to lock in and stay very focused. He has a lot of personality off the court. But when it came down to working, his work ethic is unmatche

item.title: “My son’s time in New Orleans is when he really …
item.description: “Life has a way of doing that to you and taking you full circle,” Byron Scott said. “My son’s time in New Orleans is when he really started getting the bug about getting involved in basketball. One thing about him is he’s worked his (butt) for years to get in position to do something like this. I’m so proud and happy for him."

item.title: Different possibilities are being discussed, including …
item.description: Among the most interesting outcomes that will affect the rest of the NBA will be how an expansion draft is structured -- the last time was in 2004, and it was for just one team, the Charlotte Bobcats. At that time, teams were able to protect eight players and lose only one. There is also the question of how the league will realign the conferences. Different possibilities are being discussed, including moving one team from the Western Conference to the East -- with Memphis, Minnesota and New Orle

item.title: Pelicans hosted pre-draft workouts today with Maliq …
item.description: Will Guillory: The Pelicans hosted pre-draft workouts today with Maliq Brown (Duke), Malik Dia (Ole Miss), Xavian Lee (Florida), JJ Starling (Syracuse) and Peter Suder (Miami OH), league sources tell @TheAthletic.

item.title: Trey Murphy III could be more obtainable in this …
item.description: New Orleans Pelicans wing Trey Murphy III could be more obtainable in this transaction cycle, league sources said. Murphy is a player the Warriors front office has circled for years as a prime wing who fits a need, the Kerr system and, at 25, accomplishes the goal of getting younger. The Pelicans have clung to him tightly, but league sources said New Orleans has been signaling a desire to jump into the first round of next week's draft. The Warriors have the 11th pick and control of their future

item.title: Hornets have Nic Claxton on their radar
item.description: With Charlotte considering a starting-caliber center upgrade, other centers besides Sabonis are on their radar, including Brooklyn Nets center Nic Claxton, league sources told HoopsHype. In addition, it’s worth noting that the Nets have expressed interest in Hornets free agent guard Coby White in the past when he was on the Chicago Bulls, sources said. In other Hornets news, assistant coach Zach Peterson is a candidate to become the franchise’s next head coach of their G League affiliate, the Gr

item.title: The Pelicans are known to "want a lot" for Trey …
item.description: The Pelicans are known to "want a lot" for Trey Murphy, as one interested team told The Stein Line last week, but they will at least listen. The Pelicans, furthermore, are also known to be very interested in acquiring a lottery pick for next week's festivities. New Orleans, at present, only holds No. 58 at the end of next Wednesday's second round.

item.title: Nate Ament is indeed very much one of the top …
item.description: Sources say Nate Ament is indeed very much one of the top prospects that would interest New Orleans if it could get high enough to draft him.

item.title: Pelicans have hired AJ Meyer as Director of Analytics
item.description: Shamit Dua: Pelicans have hired AJ Meyer as Director of Analytics. Meyer comes from the Suns with previous stints in Dallas and San Antonio.

item.title: Warriors still interested in Kawhi Leonard?
item.description: Kevin O'Connor says Warriors still covet Kawhi Leonard: [Anthony] Slater goes on to write how 'Trey Murphy is the more obtainable wing for the Warriors to target.' He said 'The Pelicans have clung tightly to Trey Murphy, but league sources tell Slater that New Orleans has been signaling a desire to jump into the first round of the draft.' That last line is 150% true. The Pelicans are trying to get a pick in the top 20. The Warriors are definitely a team that we could see trade the number 11 pick

item.title: Celtics targeting Trey Murphy if they don't land Giannis Antetokounmpo?
item.description: Then again, the Celtics’ desperation level simply isn’t at the level of the Heat’s. By all accounts, Boston could forge ahead with the Brown-Jayson Tatum dynamic duo and look for other ways to upgrade the frontcourt. To that end, league sources say the Celtics are among the many teams with interest in the New Orleans Pelicans’ Trey Murphy III (if they don’t land Antetokounmpo).

item.title: Dallas does not appear among Ament’s stated workouts, …
item.description: The reporting that ties Dallas to Ament comes from Jake Fischer of The Stein Line, who listed the Los Angeles Clippers (No. 5), Brooklyn Nets (No. 6), and Mavericks (No. 9) among the teams that have shown interest, with some rival executives viewing the Milwaukee Bucks at No. 10 as Ament’s floor. Fischer also linked the New Orleans Pelicans, who do not own a lottery pick, as a team interested in trading to gain positioning to select Ament. Two of the four teams Ament named, Brooklyn and the Clip

item.title: Kevin Garnett: Victor Wembanyama should focus on core strength
item.description: Kevin Garnett on Victor Wembanyama: One thing I want to speak on is just Wemby's development. If I'm him, I’m working on core strength because when… Paul will tell you, I'm not the biggest guy, right? But he'll tell you when I sit down and post up, can you move me? Paul Pierce: No, he's strong. Garnett: I'm probably one of the strongest people you could ever run up on and look like how I look, but when it came down there to setting up on that block and then pitching and getting it off down there

item.title: Sam Amick on Ja Morant: "The only two teams I have …
item.description: Sam Amick on Ja Morant: "The only two teams I have heard and I will pick one of them is New Orleans and Sacramento. I will probably push it the New Orleans way."

item.title: Boston has its eyes on Trey Murphy III and may try to …
item.description: Boston has its eyes on Trey Murphy III and may try to make a deal for the New Orleans two-way wing. The Celtics could throw a couple of first-round picks in a deal.

item.title: The Lakers have hired former New Orleans Pelicans …
item.description: Benjamin Royer: The Lakers have hired former New Orleans Pelicans basketball analytics coordinator Dominic Samangy as a basketball analyst.

item.title: The list of veterans whom the Wolves have discussed …
item.description: The list of veterans whom the Wolves have discussed this spring includes the Milwaukee Bucks’ Giannis Antetokounmpo, the Dallas Mavericks’ Kyrie Irving, the New Orleans Pelicans’ Trey Murphy III, the Chicago Bulls’ Josh Giddey, the Boston Celtics’ Derrick White and many more. A couple of important caveats are that every team in the league is considering veteran help, and that having interest in a player does not mean any potential trades have ever been close.

item.title: "The Pistons have expressed interest in Kyrie Irving. …
item.description: Marc Stein: "The Pistons have expressed interest in Kyrie Irving. Dallas is telling teams Kyrie Irving is not available. The Pistons have expressed interest in Trey Murphy, but so have a zillion other teams, and New Orleans' threshold for trading Trey Murphy away is really, really high. So the Pistons have looked at other targets. Tyler Herro, if he ends up going to Milwaukee in a Giannis trade, we reported that Thursday night, we revealed that, that the Pistons have interest in Tyler Herro, but

item.title: A lot of people are keeping an eye and monitoring the …
item.description: Shams Charania: "You definitely have to look at a couple players in New Orleans, Trey Murphy, Herb Jones. Those are two players that have gotten a ton of interest in the marketplace. A lot of people are keeping an eye and monitoring the Kawhi Leonard situation with the Clippers. He's got one year left on his deal. We don't know how that's going to play out with the Clippers. There's no urgency there, they've been through this with Kawhi before where they're entering the last year of his deal."

item.title: Pelicans have maintained an asking price of MULTIPLE …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: The Pelicans have maintained an asking price of MULTIPLE first-round picks for Trey Murphy or Herb Jones, per @JakeLFischer 6-12 teams have inquired about trading for them

item.title: "The Pelicans are trying to trade Trey Murphy. I …
item.description: Marc Spears: "The Pelicans are trying to trade Trey Murphy. I texted him the other day. He was kind of laughing about all these rumors. He's in France right now. But I do think the right, I think the thing is this: it probably will depend on who's sitting there at 11 when that pick comes up. And there's probably a certain amount of players that Joe Dumars and Troy Weaver have on their list that could be attractive to their franchise. If the Warriors can get Trey Murphy, I'm all for trading that

item.title: Pelicans have no plans to pursue Ja Morant
item.description: The Pelicans have been mentioned at various points this offseason as a potential Ja Morant trade suitor, but The Stein Line has been advised that New Orleans has no plans to pursue Memphis' very available guard. The Pelicans, as one source put it, drafted Jeremiah Fears this time last year to be their point guard of the future. Thus they are not, we're told, plotting any sort of run at Morant. New Orleans, however, does continue to receive significant external trade interest in Murphy and Herb J

item.title: Myles Turner and Kyle Kuzma trade candidates for Bucks?
item.description: With Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bobby Portis gone, all eyes turn to holdovers Myles Turner and Kyle Kuzma as trade candidates for the Bucks. Turner has always known that if Antetokounmpo was ever traded, he’d be a strong trade candidate, HoopsHype has learned. During his Pacers tenure, Turner was linked to the Hornets, Pelicans, and Lakers in trade rumors at various points. Those teams could all make sense now, and possibly the Celtics. Turner, 30, is still owed $83.55 million, including a player

item.title: If any deal with Detroit ever came to fruition, expect …
item.description: Will the Pistons make another run at Herro, who’s currently set to join his hometown Bucks? Or will Detroit attempt to pivot by making a run at Norman Powell in free agency or via trade for Trey Murphy III, whom they’ve expressed interest in? If any deal with Detroit ever came to fruition, expect Pelicans executive Troy Weaver to insist on reuniting with Stewart, one of his favorite players from his Pistons tenure.

item.title: Warriors have discussed No. 11 pick to Pelicans for Trey Murphy
item.description: Golden State has also been one of many interested suitors in New Orleans Pelicans forward Trey Murphy III, and the two teams have discussed concepts involving the No. 11 pick, league sources told HoopsHype. As previously reported by HoopsHype, the Pelicans have searched for a trade into the lottery and are targeting a player. Nate Ament has been mentioned as one potential candidate for the Pelicans. Before this past trade deadline, several teams, including the Warriors, Pistons, Pacers, Hawks, T

item.title: “[The] Brooklyn [Nets] and the [Los Angeles] Clippers …
item.description: Sam Amick: “[The] Brooklyn [Nets] and the [Los Angeles] Clippers were mentioned as teams that were out front in that race, but I think the list is very long. I think that Detroit comes to mind. [With] Boston, actually, Trey is seen as a potential plan B for them if they miss out on Giannis, which they obviously did. So, Trey is going to have a lot of suitors, and I think that could be a draft-night deal. “There was even a scenario floating around with Trey going to the [Atlanta] Hawks in a deal

item.title: Lakers among teams that reached out about Yves Missi
item.description: Multiple teams, including the Lakers, have reached out to the Pelicans about Yves Missi, but the Pelicans have resisted those offers. Also worth noting on New Orleans: the club had been exploring its options to move up in the first round of the draft. Some members of the organization are fans of Nate Ament.

item.title: League sources say Golden State and Atlanta are …
item.description: A subplot I'm watching tonight: Will Trey Murphy III finally get traded out of New Orleans? There has been all kinds of chatter about the 26-year-old small forward being on the move of late, with a laundry list of teams interested. League sources say Golden State and Atlanta are currently vetting scenarios in which they'd trade their pick (No. 8 and No. 11, respectively) to land him. He's been tied to Brooklyn (pre-Julius Randle trade), the Clippers (No. 5 pick) and Boston (No. 27) as well.

item.title: Pelicans draft Jaron Pierre Jr at No. 58
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The New Orleans Pelicans will select Jaron Pierre Jr. with the 58th pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: Sources: East Carolina’s Jordan Riley will sign an NBA …
item.description: Billy Reinhardt: Sources: East Carolina’s Jordan Riley will sign an NBA contract and play in Summer League with the New Orleans Pelicans. Agent Jason Rich of TCA Next negotiated the deal.

item.title: Tre White and Melvin Council Jr. have signed free …
item.description: Former University of Kansas men’s basketball players Tre White and Melvin Council Jr. have signed free agent deals with NBA teams after not being selected in the two-round NBA Draft, KU confirmed in a pair of posts on social media site X on Thursday morning. White, a 6-foot-7 wing from Dallas, has signed a deal with the Miami Heat. He initially reported the news with 247Sports via a social media post. Council, a 6-4 guard from Rochester, New York, has signed with the New Orleans Pelicans. Counci

item.title: Shawn Phillips Jr. has signed a Summer League deal …
item.description: Dushawn London: Missouri big man Shawn Phillips Jr. has signed a Summer League deal with the New Orleans Pelicans, source tells @247Sports

item.title: Pelicans exercised 2026-27 team option on Karlo Matkovic
item.description: Michael Scotto: The New Orleans Pelicans have exercised their third-year, $2.30 million team option for the 2026-27 season on forward Karlo Matković. He averaged 5.7 points on 60.4% shooting from the field and 42.2% from 3-point range with 3.7 rebounds in 62 games played last season.

item.title: Pelicans have tendered a two-way qualifying offer to …
item.description: Keith Smith: The New Orleans Pelicans have tendered a two-way qualifying offer to Hunter Dickinson, a league source told @spotrac. Dickinson will now be a restricted free agent this summer.

item.title: Melvin Council Jr. has agreed to sign with the …
item.description: Undrafted rookie guard Melvin Council Jr. has agreed to sign with the Pelicans, according to his agency, Seros Partners (Instagram link). Jon Chepkevich of DraftExpress, who is tracking undrafted free agent deals at RookieScale.com, confirmed to Hoops Rumors that the deal will be an Exhibit 10.

item.title: Pelicans to decline team option on Kevon Looney
item.description: Chris Haynes: New Orleans Pelicans are expected to decline the $8M team option on veteran center Kevon Looney, making the 3-time NBA champ an unrestricted free agent, league sources tell me.

item.title: Mavs interested in adding Willie Green to Dusty May's staff
item.description: Marc Stein: Former Pelicans coach Willie Green, who has been pursued by Golden State to rejoin Steve Kerr's staff as an assistant, also has interest from Dallas as new Mavericks coach Dusty May assembles his first NBA staff, league sources say. Former Bucks coach Adrian Griffin and Heat consultant Noah LaRoche have likewise been mentioned as expected candidates for May's bench in Dallas. As @TheSteinLine previously reported, LaRoche was part of the monthlong interview process that eventually led

item.title: While the Pelicans hope to retain Bey going forward, …
item.description: While the Pelicans hope to retain Bey going forward, the same can’t be said for assistant coach Jodie Meeks, who won’t return to New Orleans next season, league sources told HoopsHype. Meeks was with the Birmingham Squadron, the G League affiliate for New Orleans, from 2022-24 before moving up as an assistant coach on former head coach Willie Green’s staff.

item.title: Pelicans, Rockets, Magic not in the mix for Jaylen Brown
item.description: Other teams, such as the New Orleans Pelicans, Houston Rockets, and Orlando Magic, who’ve been loosely linked to Brown, have been described as not in the mix.

item.title: Saddiq Bey, Pelicans interested in an extension
item.description: Saddiq Bey is coming off the best season of his career with the New Orleans Pelicans and now both sides are interested in an extension, league sources told HoopsHype. He is eligible to sign one as early as July 11. After a torn ACL that sidelined him for the entire 2024-25 season, the 27-year-old forward averaged career highs in points (17.7) and field goal percentage (.451), while starting 64 of 72 games played. Bey was drafted in Detroit by current Pelicans executive Troy Weaver, who also trad

item.title: Karlo Matkovic: ‘The role of a veteran is very …
item.description: Karlo Matkovic: ‘The role of a veteran is very important, and DeAndre Jordan really helped me a lot this year. It helps to have a team like that where someone doesn't just come to me and say 'Cheers' and then ignore me. He learned from Jokić, he learned from Dončić. (…) He really turned out to be an 'extra' veteran. I go with him after games; he calls me, we joke around, it's great. But you can also see how he maintains his body—it’s unreal.’

item.title: Whether Bronny is a professional at that level yet is …
item.description: Karlo Matkovic on LeBron James: ‘He literally made a son to be his teammate. People talk about him and say he’s not good, but at the very least, he brought his son into the league. LeBron said he can play, he said '100% believe me.' And Bronny is not bad in the G League; he is strong, even though he is small. You have people like Brunson saying from the side that he thinks he can't make it, but all those haters... everyone has their own opinion. It's a unique situation in the league. You have to

item.title: Pelicans general manager Joe Dumars has been pushing …
item.description: League sources say multiple teams have put two first-round picks on the table for Murphy. Pelicans general manager Joe Dumars has been pushing for three firsts in discussions so far, and there is still a chance he gets there. But for now, teams appear to be holding firm at two.

item.title: Knicks to pursue Kevon Looney?
item.description: If Robinson does, in fact, depart New York amid the Knicks' well-chronicled financial constraints as the dreaded second apron closes in, sources say that the newly crowned champions are expected to try to bring on a veteran center with plenty of playoff experience: Former Warriors big man Kevon Looney.

item.title: DeAndre Jordan re-signs with Pelicans
item.description: Chris Haynes: Free agent center DeAndre Jordan is re-signing with the New Orleans Pelicans for what will be his 19th NBA season, league sources tell me.

item.title: DeAndre Jordan's deal with the Pelicans is a fully …
item.description: ‪Shamit Dua‬: Per league sources, DeAndre Jordan's deal with the Pelicans is a fully guaranteed minimum

item.title: We tried to get Eric Gordon to take $40 million, since …
item.description: We had a meeting at the first possible moment — 9 p.m. in L.A. — with little-used Indiana Pacers forward Solomon Hill, and realized our hopes of getting him for “just” four years and $40 million were unrealistic given the multiple rivals lining up to beat our offer (New Orleans eventually got him for four years and $52 million). We tried to get Eric Gordon to take $40 million, since he’d known our star guard, Mike Conley, since childhood; we didn’t know he had more than $50 million waiting for h

item.title: Willie Green expected to join Mavs as top assistant coach
item.description: Marc Stein: The Mavericks, I’m told, are expected to secure former Pelicans coach Willie Green as a top assistant for Dusty May’s first NBA coaching staff. The Warriors hoped to bring him back for a second stint on Steve Kerr’s staff but May’s jump to the NBA gave Green two strong options.

item.title: Pelicans reject Knicks attempts to trade for Yves Missi
item.description: Chris Haynes: Sources: New York Knicks have tried feverishly to trade for New Orleans Pelicans center Yves Missi after losing Mitchell Robinson, but offers have been rejected multiple times. Pelicans view Missi as a vital core figure and the message is that he’s unavailable.

item.title: Pelicans signaled that Yves Missi would not be …
item.description: Ian Begley: Around the NBA Draft, Pelicans signaled that Yves Missi would not be available via trade and will in New Orleans under new head coach Jamahl Mosely

item.title: Trey Murphy drawing trade interest from Celtics, Pistons, Warriors, Clippers, Hawks
item.description: Teams that are in the mix for Murphy to varying degrees now are the Celtics, Pistons, Warriors, Clippers, and Hawks, league sources told HoopsHype. The Celtics have monitored Murphy’s trade market, and following the Brown trade for Paul George, two first-round picks, and two second-round picks, they have gained extra draft capital that New Orleans has desired in any trade talks. The Pistons have consistently circled the wagons on Murphy in trade talks, but felt the price was too high. With the a

item.title: My job as a coach is to bring what's in him out into …
item.description: Pelicans Film Room: "Just working on all the skills he already has. My job as a coach is to bring what's in him out into the light. I can't put something in you that's not in you. He has all the tools, all the awareness, and the work ethic" -- God Shammgod on working with Jeremiah Fears

item.title: The asking price for Trey Murphy was three first-round …
item.description: Dalton Johnson: The asking price for Trey Murphy was three first-round picks at the trade deadline. The hope would be two But you do what it takes to get a talent like Trey. He's the perfect bridge the Warriors need, and have needed. Do what it takes, as every team should be doing with him

item.title: Boston has kicked the tires on Pelicans forward Trey …
item.description: The Celtics can spin that the pair of first-rounders they picked up have value. And they do. The 2028 one will likely end up as the Clippers’, who are beginning a rebuild with an Aspiration-sized anvil over their head. The ’31 pick is Philadelphia’s and, hey, who knows where the Sixers will be in four years. Boston has kicked the tires on Pelicans forward Trey Murphy III, sources say. This deal gives them more assets to pursue him.

item.title: "Maybe they end up rerouting Paul George elsewhere. …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Jovan: "Maybe they end up rerouting Paul George elsewhere. There's been talks about maybe them doing something with New Orleans. They have multiple firsts to put on the table to maybe go after Trey Murphy. Barring that this is a pretty surprising return for a guy of Jaylen Brown's caliber"

item.title: Garrett Temple retires after 16 NBA seasons, joins Mavericks coaching staff
item.description: Marc Stein: UPDATE: Garrett Temple is joining Dusty May's coaching staff in Dallas, Dan Eveloff of @PrioritySports confirms to @TheSteinLine. After a 16-year playing career, Temple has accepted May's pitch to move into coaching on a staff that also features ex-Pelicans coach Willie Green.

item.title: Most of the deals involving Murphy have been about a …
item.description: Most of the deals involving Murphy have been about a deal to the Atlanta Hawks, a team in need of another high-level perimeter scorer on the wing. And most of the trades have included Murphy going to Atlanta in exchange for some sort of package centered around reigning Most Improved Player, Nickeil Alexander-Walker, and former lottery pick big man Onyeka Okongwu.

item.title: Pelicans would need to be blown away to trade Trey Murphy?
item.description: Shamit Dua: On the Trey Murphy front, I'm told that while the Pelicans are fielding a steady stream of inbound calls, they have made it clear they'll only listen if they are blown away. Pelicans are content to keep Murphy and are not actively shopping him

item.title: The Pelicans have hired Dr. Matt Taberner as Director …
item.description: Shamit Dua: The Pelicans have hired Dr. Matt Taberner as Director of Innovation, Science and Reconditioning

item.title: Taberner has done stints at Everton, the Orlando …
item.description: Shamit Dua: Taberner has done stints at Everton, the Orlando Magic, and Manchester City (Academy). Taberner has a doctorate in applied sport and exercise science from Liverpool John Moores University. Like David Cosgrave, extensive experience in professional soccer

item.title: Hunter Dickinson re-signs with New Orleans
item.description: Hunter Dickinson is staying in the NBA. The former Michigan star signed a two-way contract with the New Orleans Pelicans, he announced on his social media. Dickinson appeared in five games for the Pelicans as a rookie last season.

item.title: For them to give me that call, it was emotional.
item.description: Pelicans Film Room: "It's surreal. Growing up in New Orleans Lousiana, you want to play for LSU, the Pelicans, the Saints. For them to give me that call, it was emotional. Me and my mom shed a tear. It's an opportunity to get better" -- Solomon Washington on getting the call from the Pelicans

item.title: Jamahl Mosley says a big reason Fears and Queen are …
item.description: Rod Walker: Jamahl Mosley says a big reason Fears and Queen are not playing Summer League is they played 82 and 81 games, respectively, this season. They have been around working out with the team though.

item.title: Level. I think one thing that we want to make sure we …
item.description: New Orleans Pelicans head coach Jamahl Mosley recently expressed confidence in the roster. (via New Orleans Pelicans): “I think there’s 30 teams right now that are saying that they can compete at a high level. I think one thing that we want to make sure we do here is we just let our actions do the talking, and I think that’s what we’re gonna have to do. We’re gonna work every single night. We’re gonna play extremely hard every single night. We’re gonna play together, play for each other. So what

item.title: Hunter Dickinson has signed a contract to return to …
item.description: Hunter Dickinson has signed a contract to return to the New Orleans Pelicans on a two-way contract. Dickinson spent his rookie season with the Pelicans on a two-way deal.

item.title: Zion Williamson, Jordan Poole, Yves Missi, Herb Jones, …
item.description: Will Guillory: Zion Williamson, Jordan Poole, Yves Missi, Herb Jones, Jeremiah Fears, Derik Queen and DeAndre Jordan are among the Pelican players in attendance for today's game

item.title: Dejounte Murray: "You got to lead by example. You got …
item.description: "You're sitting next to Zion there, you guys are the veteran leaders now of this group. How do you feel like you can lead the two of you this Pelicans squad this year?" Dejounte Murray: "You got to lead by example. You got to be on time, you got to be the ultimate pro, put your work in day in, day out, take care of your body. Those unseen hours, when you're in your hotel room, when you're on the recovery table, you got to take care of yourself so we're available. The best ability is availability

item.title: Besiktas Istanbul have announced another addition …
item.description: Besiktas Istanbul have announced another addition ahead of their long-awaited return to the EuroLeague next season. The club officially confirmed the signing of former NBA guard Jaylen Nowell. The 27-year-old has appeared in more than 200 NBA games, representing the Minnesota Timberwolves, Memphis Grizzlies, Detroit Pistons, and New Orleans Pelicans, among other teams.
